title: Support u128::MAX in the lexer
description: The lexer currently parses numeric literals into i128, so u128::MAX (which exceeds i128::MAX) overflows with 'number too large to fit in target type'. Values up to u64::MAX work. To support u128::MAX, the lexer needs either: (a) a separate Token::UInt(u128) variant, or (b) a single Token::Int(u128) with a sign flag, or (c) fall back from i128 parse to u128 parse and store as bit-cast i128 (with deserialize_u128 doing an unsigned cast back). Currently marked with #[ignore] in tests/edge_cases.rs::numbers::u128_max. Low priority — u128 is rarely used in configs.
close_reason: Fixed: added Token::UInt(u128) variant used only when value exceeds i128::MAX; parse_int_literal() tries i128 first, falls back to u128; int_to_u128() helper handles both variants with sign checking (rejects negative Token::Int); all 10 deserialize_i*/u* methods updated. 3 new edge-case tests cover u128::MAX, i128::MAX+1 boundary, and negative-literal rejection for unsigned. 137 tests pass; clippy clean; nix flake check passes.
